task:
Implement the CodeRabbit Coding Plan for issue #2571: fix: Outline → Storyboard transition — submit button greyed out ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/2025slideheroes-sophie` 2. `git fetch upstream && git push origin upstream/dev:dev --force` 3. `git checkout upstream/dev && git checkout -b sophie/issue-2571` 4. Read the full issue: `gh issue view 2571 --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes` 5. Implement according to the Coding Plan below 6. Run `pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck` before committing 7. Commit with: `git commit -m "feat: fix: Outline → Storyboard transition — submit button greyed out (#2571)"` 8. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-2571` 9. Open PR on the **fork** for CodeRabbit review (NOT on upstream): ```bash gh pr create --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes-sophie \ --base dev \ --head sophie/issue-2571 \ --title "feat: fix: Outline → Storyboard transition — submit button greyed out" \ --body "Implements slideheroes/2025slideheroes#2571 Implemented per CodeRabbit Coding Plan. > This PR is on the fork for CodeRabbit review. It will be promoted to upstream automatically once CR is satisfied and CI passes." ``` **IMPORTANT:** - Open the PR on the FORK (`slideheroes/2025slideheroes-sophie`), NOT on upstream (`slideheroes/2025slideheroes`). - The PR will be automatically promoted to upstream after CodeRabbit review. - Do NOT open a PR on upstream directly. ## CodeRabbit Coding Plan ## Coding Plan ### Summary - The root cause is a stale client-side prop (`completedSteps`) that never updates after the server action writes to the database - Fix introduces a React context to hold `completedSteps` as reactive client state, initialized from SSR props - On successful save, `markStepComplete` updates the context immediately, enabling the Continue button - Server-side `revalidatePath` is added for consistency on full page refreshes ## Implementation Steps ### Phase 1: Create Client-Side Step Progress Context Introduce a React context to hold `completedSteps` as reactive client-side state. This allows child components (including `ContinueButton`) to observe real-time changes after a save action succeeds.
Create StepProgressContext Define a context that holds the current `completedSteps` array and exposes a `markStepComplete(stepName)` function to add steps dynamically. - Create new file `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/[id]/_components/step-progress-context.tsx` - Export `StepProgressProvider` component that accepts initial `completedSteps` prop and manages state via `useState` - Export `useStepProgress` hook that returns `{ completedSteps, markStepComplete }` - `markStepComplete` should be idempotent (no-op if step already in array)
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` Create a new React context file for tracking step completion state client-side. The goal is to lift `completedSteps` out of a static prop and into reactive state. - Create `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/[id]/_components/step-progress-context.tsx` - Define a context with shape `{ completedSteps: string[], markStepComplete: (stepName: string) => void }` - Export `StepProgressProvider` component: - Accepts an initial `completedSteps` prop (string array from SSR) - Manages state internally via `useState`, initialized from that prop - Provides context value to children - Export `useStepProgress` hook that reads from the context - `markStepComplete` must be idempotent — skip the update if the step is already present in the array ```
### Phase 2: Integrate Context into Workflow Shell Wire the context into the existing component hierarchy so both child pages and `ContinueButton` can access the reactive state.
Task 1: Wrap WorkflowShell with StepProgressProvider Modify `workflow-shell.tsx` to wrap its entire return JSX with `StepProgressProvider`, initializing from the SSR `completedSteps` prop. - Update `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/[id]/_components/workflow-shell.tsx` - Import and wrap JSX with `` - This ensures both `{props.children}` (step pages) and `ContinueButton` share the same context
Task 2: Update ContinueButton to Read from Context Change `ContinueButton`'s enabled logic to read from context rather than a direct prop. - Update `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/[id]/_components/workflow-shell.tsx` - Replace `enabled={props.completedSteps.includes(currentStep)}` with context-based check - Use `useStepProgress()` to get live `completedSteps` array
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` Wire the `StepProgressContext` into `WorkflowShell` so both child pages and `ContinueButton` share the same reactive state. Two changes are needed in `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/[id]/_components/workflow-shell.tsx`: - Import `StepProgressProvider` and `useStepProgress` from the newly created context file - Wrap the entire return JSX of `WorkflowShell` with `` — this seeds client state from the SSR prop - Inside `WorkflowShell`, call `useStepProgress()` to get the live `completedSteps` array - Replace the static `enabled={props.completedSteps.includes(currentStep)}` on `ContinueButton` with the context-derived value ```
### Phase 3: Trigger Step Completion on Successful Save Hook into the existing save mutation's success callback to update the client-side context immediately after the database is updated.
Call markStepComplete in OutlineEditor's onSuccess Add context update to the save callback so the button enables the moment the save succeeds. - Update `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/[id]/outline/_components/outline-editor.tsx` - Import `useStepProgress` and call `markStepComplete('outline')` inside the `onSuccess` callback of `handleSave` (around line 217) - This must happen after the server action returns successfully
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` Update the outline editor to notify the context immediately when a save succeeds — this is what actually unblocks the Continue button without requiring a page reload. - Update `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/[id]/outline/_components/outline-editor.tsx` - Import `useStepProgress` from the context file - Destructure `markStepComplete` from `useStepProgress()` - In the `onSuccess` callback of `handleSave` (around line 217), call `markStepComplete('outline')` — this must execute only after the server action returns successfully, not optimistically ```
### Phase 4: Add Server-Side Cache Invalidation Ensure the SSR cache is invalidated after save so that page refreshes and navigations see the correct state.
Add revalidatePath to save-outline.action.ts Add Next.js cache invalidation to keep server and client in sync. - Update `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/[id]/outline/_actions/save-outline.action.ts` - Import `revalidatePath` from `next/cache` - Call `revalidatePath(\`/home/ai/${data.presentationId}/outline\`)` before returning success (after line 77) - This follows the established pattern used elsewhere in the codebase (e.g., storyboar ## Coding Rules # Coding Rules These rules document the actual code quality patterns used in the SlideHeroes codebase. ## TypeScript Patterns - Strict mode compliance: enable all strict TypeScript options - Use explicit local type aliases rather than inline complex types - Use `z.infer` for Zod-derived types - Use `instanceof Error` narrowing in catch blocks instead of `instanceof` checks on non-Error values - Never use `any` - use `unknown` with type guards when needed ## API Route Patterns - Use Next.js 15 style with `params` as Promise - Use named exports (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE) for route handlers - Use `.catch(() => ({}))` for body parsing error handling - Use `NextResponse.json({ error })` response shape - Validate input at boundaries using Zod schemas ## Prisma Patterns - Import Prisma client from `@/lib/db` - Use conditional spread for PATCH updates (e.g., `...(data || {})`) - Use `.catch(() => null)` for not-found handling ## Import Conventions - Use `@/` alias for src imports (e.g., `@/components/Button`) - Import from specific files, avoid barrel exports - Separate client and server imports - never mix them from the same file ## Error Handling - Use tight try/catch around specific I/O operations only - Use `logger.error({ err }, '...')` for structured logging - Import logger from `@kit/shared/logger` - Handle errors gracefully using try/catch and appropriate error types - Use `isRedirectError` from `next/dist/client/components/redirect-error` for client-side redirect handling ## React Patterns - Encapsulate repeated blocks of code into reusable local components - Write small, composable, explicit, well-named components - Always use `react-hook-form` and `@kit/ui/form` for forms - Use client components only when needed for interactivity (use `use client` directive) - Add `data-testid` for E2E tests where appropriate - Prefer server-side data fetching using RSC - Display loading indicators where appropriate ## Next.js Patterns - Use `enhanceAction` for Server Actions from `@kit/next/actions` - Use `use server` in server action files - Export page components using the `withI18n` utility - Add well-written page metadata to pages - Redirect using `redirect` following a server action instead of using client-side router ## Data Fetching Patterns - Use async server components that call loader functions for initial data fetching - Use `enhanceAction` for create/update/delete operations with schema validation - Use `Promise.all()` for parallel data fetching in Server Components ## Linting and Formatting Before committing, always run: ```bash pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck ``` ## Component Organization - Route-specific components: `_components/` directories - Route utilities: `_lib/` for client, `_lib/server/` for server-side - Global components: Root-level directories ## Naming Conventions - Pages: `page.tsx` (Next.js convention) - Loaders: `{feature}-page.loader.ts` - Actions: `{feature}-server-actions.ts` - Schemas: `{feature}.schema.ts` - Components: `kebab-case.tsx`
label: neo-issue-2571
